evals.prompt: I have a UserService class in TypeScript with methods for createUser, updateUser, deleteUser, and getUserById. It talks to a PostgreSQL database through Prisma and sends welcome emails via SendGrid. There are zero tests. Help me add a comprehensive test suite.
evals.expected_output: Should assess the current state, map behaviors for each method, separate unit vs integration concerns, recommend mocking strategy for Prisma and SendGrid, and write structured tests grouped by behavior.
assertions: Asks about or checks existing test infrastructure before writing tests
Maps behaviors for each method (not just happy paths)
Separates unit tests (business logic) from integration tests (database queries)
Recommends mocking Prisma client for unit tests
Recommends wrapping SendGrid and mocking the wrapper
Groups tests by behavior (describe blocks), not by method only
Includes error path tests (user not found, duplicate email, email send failure)
Does NOT use TDD red-green-refactor cycle — writes tests for existing code

evals.prompt: Our Django project has 0% test coverage and we need to get to a reasonable level before a big refactor. We have about 30 models, 50 views, and 20 utility functions. Where do I even start?
evals.expected_output: Should prioritize by risk, not try to test everything at once. Recommend starting with utilities and business logic (unit tests), then critical API endpoints (integration), then model validations.
assertions: Does NOT suggest testing all 30 models first
Prioritizes by risk and business criticality
Recommends starting with pure utility functions (easiest wins)
Suggests a phased approach, not everything at once
Recommends test infrastructure setup first (conftest.py, factories)
References the testing pyramid (70/20/10 ratio)
Mentions that this coverage enables the refactoring skill to work safely

evals.expected_output: Should create a comprehensive behavior map first, then write tests covering all branches including edge cases and error paths. Should use descriptive test names and group by scenario.
assertions: Creates a behavior map before writing tests
Tests empty items array returns 0
Tests domestic weight boundaries (< 1, < 5, >= 5)
Tests the incremental pricing formula for heavy domestic orders
Tests international linear pricing
Tests the error case for unknown destination
Tests with null/undefined items
Uses descriptive test names that read as specifications
Does NOT just write one test per if-branch — tests boundary values
